genres.name: Gastronomic Curiosity
genres.description: Deep dives into unusual ingredients or the science of taste, blending factual curiosity with a touch of the macabre or the unexpected.
examples: Inside The Curious World Of Porcupine Meat
genres.structure: Provocative hook → The 'What/Why' → Scientific or Cultural Breakdown → Warning/Verdict → Final Reflection
genres.prompt_instruction: Balance a sense of adventure with precise factual details. Use evocative adjectives to describe textures and risks, framing the food as a 'curiosity' rather than just a meal.

genres.name: Chef Profile
genres.description: Character-driven portraits of culinary figures, focusing on their life trajectory and philosophical approach to cooking.
examples: Chef Adek: The Many Lives Of Datin Hamidah Abdul Hamid
Unfiltered: A Candid Table With Chef Son Jong-Won And Chef Masashi Horiuchi
genres.structure: Atmospheric intro → Biographical arc → Professional philosophy → Dialogue/Quotes → Legacy
genres.prompt_instruction: Focus on the 'human' side of the chef. Use a narrative arc that treats the chef's career as a series of 'lives' or chapters, emphasizing personal evolution over simple recipes.

genres.name: Lifestyle & Home Guide
genres.description: Practical, accessible advice for the home cook or epicurean, often framed as 'simple' or 'affordable' hacks.
genres.structure: Problem/Desire → The Solution/Guide → Step-by-step/List → Pro-Tip → Closing encouragement
genres.prompt_instruction: Keep it punchy and utilitarian. Frame the advice as a personal recommendation ('My Simple Guide') to establish trust and accessibility.

genres.name: Debunking Explainer
genres.description: Comedic myth-busting pieces that challenge conventional wisdom, written in direct second-person with an imaginary sceptic as the foil.
examples: You've Been Lied To: The Truth About Vegetables
The Myth Of The Perfect Cup Of Coffee
genres.structure: Provocative claim → Address the sceptic → Evidence per item → Philosophical pivot → Wry concession
genres.prompt_instruction: Write as if explaining something obvious to a room full of politely incredulous people. The humour comes from patience and precision, not from exaggeration. Use parenthetical asides. Address the reader directly. Never shout.

genres.name: Venue Spotlight
genres.description: Reviews or introductions of new dining establishments, blending the ambiance and the menu's unique selling points.
examples: Mesa On 51 Brings Elevated Spanish-Asian Fusion To KL’s Skyline
Shuruti Box: The South Indian Fusion & Tamil Bar Redefining KL's Scene
genres.structure: The Vibe/Setting → The Concept → Signature Dishes → The 'Why it Matters' (Impact on scene) → Conclusion
genres.prompt_instruction: Use 'elevated' language to describe the setting. Focus on the fusion elements and how the venue 'redefines' or 'adds' to the existing city skyline/scene.

tones.name: Intellectual & Curious
tones.description: An inquisitive tone that explores the 'why' behind the food, often blending science and culture.

tones.name: Intimate & Respectful
tones.description: A narrative tone used for interviews and profiles, treating subjects with dignity and focus on their life journey.

tones.name: Approachable & Practical
tones.description: Conversational and helpful, stripping away pretense to make luxury or complex cooking accessible.
markers: Easy [X] you can make at home

tones.name: Sophisticated & Descriptive
tones.description: Polished and evocative, utilizing a high-end vocabulary to match the luxury of the venues described.

historical_depth.label: No history — get straight to the point
Deep cultural history — origin stories, class, colonial context

pov.label: First person — I am here, I tried this

opening_hook_types.label: The Paradox
opening_hook_types.description: Starting with a contradiction to grab attention.
opening_hook_types.example: Five reasons why [Expensive Item A] is actually more expensive than [Expensive Item B].

opening_hook_types.label: The Personal Confession
opening_hook_types.description: Using a first-person admission to build a bridge to the reader.
opening_hook_types.example: 3 Confessions of an incurable cafe romantic.

opening_hook_types.label: The Definitive Claim
opening_hook_types.description: Establishing a strong, authoritative stance on a topic immediately.

never_does: Use overly aggressive clickbait adjectives like 'Shocking' or 'Unbelievable'
Write purely instructional recipes without a narrative or philosophical framing
always_does: Frames food within a broader cultural or scientific context
Uses 'The [X] of [Y]' titling structures for profiles
Connects local Malaysian dining experiences to a wider regional or global standard

article_type_prompts.gastronomic-curiosity: Write as a culinary explorer. Start with a high-stakes or high-interest hook about a specific ingredient. Blend technical explanations (like how capsaicin works) with vivid, sensory descriptions of the eating experience, maintaining a balance between academic curiosity and gourmet appreciation.
article_type_prompts.chef-profile: Write a narrative biography. Instead of a resume of achievements, frame the chef's life as a series of transformations. Use 'candid' and 'unfiltered' language to make the reader feel they are sitting at the table with the chef, focusing on the intersection of their personal history and their culinary style.
article_type_prompts.lifestyle-guide: Write as a knowledgeable friend who knows the shortcuts. Keep the tone encouraging and light. Focus on the 'hack' or the 'alternative'—how to get a high-end result with lower effort or cost, using direct, simple language and a structured list format.
article_type_prompts.debunking-explainer: Write as someone who has done the research and is mildly exasperated that nobody else has. Use a conversational second-person voice throughout — 'you point to the carrots', 'you're probably looking at me like I've lost my mind'. Include at least two parenthetical asides for dry comedy. Build the argument item by item, then pivot to a broader philosophical observation about how humans construct shared fictions. End with wry acceptance, not outrage.
article_type_prompts.venue-spotlight: Write as a tastemaker. Focus on the synergy between the location (e.g., KL's skyline) and the cuisine. Use adjectives that suggest elevation and innovation, and explain exactly why this specific venue fills a gap in the current dining landscape.
